name: Fajita Marinade
recipeIngredient: 4 cups light soy sauce (the dark, imported Chinese kind is too strong)
1 skirt steak for each of 3 guests (4- to 6-ounce portions)
prepared pico de gallo or picante sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic powder, onion powder, lemon juice, and ginger in a jar. Shake well to mix and dissolve the sugar.
2. Let the marinade stand in a sealed jar overnight. Reserve about 1 cup.
3. Pour the remainder of the marinade over the beef and marinate, covered, for 2 hours or overnight in the refrigerator.
4. Remove the beef from the marinade and grill over very hot coals for a short time. It should take only about 10 minutes per steak if the meat is 3/4-inch thick or less.
5. Brush the meat with the reserved marinade two or three times while cooking.
6. Chop the meat with a cleaver into bite-size pieces.
7. Fold the meat into warm flour tortillas and serve with pico de gallo.
